latest.title: New Convertible Loan Note Facility
latest.content:
Today 16:18
RNS Number : 0403L
Critical Metals PLC
03 July 2026
Critical Metals plc / EPIC: CRTM / Market: Main Market
3 July 2026
Critical Metals plc
("Critical Metals" or the "Company")
New Convertible Loan Note Facility
Critical Metals plc (LSE:CRTM), a mining company established to acquire mining opportunities in the critical and strategic metals sector, which operates the Molulu Copper/Cobalt Project ("Molulu") in the Democratic Republic of Congo, announces that it has agreed with NIU Invest SE ("NIU"), the Company's majority shareholder, that it will subscribe for £2,500,000 convertible loan notes under a new convertible loan note instrument ("CLN Instrument") on similar terms to the £2,100,000 convertible loan instrument that the Company created in December 2025 ("2025 CLN Instrument"). The Company has not issued all of notes under this instrument and the remaining notes will be subscribed for by NIU at par by the end of September 2026. The funds raised under the CLN Instrument and the 2025 CLN Instrument is intended to provide the Company with the capital it needs for at least the next 12 months and will be used to explore acquisition opportunities in the mining sector, fund exploration and/or production activities at Molulu and for general working capital.
On 3 July 2026 NIU agreed to subscribed for £2,500,000 of convertible loan notes issued under the 2025 CLN Instrument at par at the following times:
(i)
£1,100,000 on or before 31 December 2026;
(ii)
£1,000,000 on or before 31 May 2027; and
(iii)
£400,000 to applied to repay the sums due under the facility provided to the Company by NIU in July 2025.
The main terms of the 2025 CLN Instrument is as follows:
·
Mature on 18 month from issue of the relevant notes;
·
Interest at 10% per annum, payable on maturity;
·
Are convertible into ordinary shares of the company at a conversation price of 10.25 per Ordinary Share of £0.0005 each in the capital of the Company ("Ordinary Shares"), which is the same conversion price as under the CLN instrument; and
·
May be redeemed at the noteholder's option at any time subject to certain restrictions and will be repayable in cash at the end of the term.
·
There are certain customary events resulting in immediate redemption related to solvency type issues.
The issue of the notes under the 2025 CLN Instrument to NIU is a material related party transaction for the purposes of DTR 7.3.6 due to NIU's shareholding in the Company representing in excess of 60.00% of the issued Ordinary Shares.
Danilo Lange, CEO of Critical Metals, said:
"We are pleased to have secured a further commitment from NIU to provide capital to help us develop the Company's business."
**ENDS**
For further information on the Company please visit
www.criticalmetals.co.uk
or contact:
Critical Metals plc
Danilo Lange, CEO
critical@stbridespartners.co.uk
St Brides Partners Ltd
Financial PR
Ana Ribeiro/Charlotte Page
critical@stbridespartners.co.uk
About Critical Metals
London listed Critical Metals plc is focused on identifying low CAPEX and OPEX brown-field projects with near-term production and cash-flow, concentrating on minerals that have strategic importance to future global economic growth. In line with this, and with an off-take partner already in place, the Company is currently focused on recommencing production at the formerly producing Molulu Copper/Cobalt Project in the Katangan Copperbelt in Democratic Republic of Congo ('DRC').
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Board Changes
latest.content:
Today 10:07
RNS Number : 9789D
Critical Metals PLC
12 May 2026
Critical Metals plc / EPIC: CRTM / Market: Main Market
12 May 2026
Critical Metals Plc
("Critical Metals" or the "Company")
Board Changes
Critical Metals plc (
LSE:CRTM)
, a mining company established to acquire mining opportunities in the critical and strategic metals sector, currently developing the Molulu Copper/Cobalt Project ("Molulu") in the Democratic Republic of Congo, announces the permanent appointment of Mr Danilo Lange as Chief Executive Officer, with immediate effect.
Mr Lange, an experienced international executive with over 25 years of leadership experience across mining, consumer goods, and marketing sectors, had served as the Company's Interim CEO since December 2025.
Kelvin Williams, Non-Executive Chairman, said:
"
We are very pleased that Danilo has agreed to continue as Chief Executive Officer on a permanent basis following his appointment as interim CEO in December 2025. During this period, he has demonstrated a clear understanding of the business. His experience and performance in the role made him the best candidate for the role."
Mr Danilo Lange, CEO of Critical Metals plc, added:
"I am delighted to be appointed as the CEO of Critical Metals on a permanent basis. I would like to take the opportunity to thank the board for their support and look forward to advancing on our stated strategy, to invest in low-capex, close to production projects with exposure to critical metals."
**ENDS**
Critical Metals plc
critical@stbridespartners.co.uk
St Brides Partners Ltd
Financial PR
Ana Ribeiro / Charlotte Page
critical@stbridespartners.co.uk
About Critical Metals
Critical Metals plc (LSE: CRTM)
is
a mining company established to acquire and invest in brownfield mining projects with low-CAPEX and OPEX, close to production, in the critical and strategic metals sector. The Company
is focused in developing the formerly producing Molulu Copper/Cobalt Project in the Katangan Copperbelt in Democratic Republic of Congo. Its shares are admitted to the Official List and trade on the Main Market of the LSE.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
Today 14:26
RNS Number : 7200T
Critical Metals PLC
19 February 2026
Critical Metals plc / EPIC: CRTM / Market: Main Market
19 February 2026
Critical Metals plc
("Critical Metals" or the "Company")
Results of AGM
Critical Metals plc, a mining company established to acquire mining opportunities in the critical and strategic metals sector, currently developing the Molulu Copper/ Cobalt Project in the Democratic Republic of Congo ("DRC"), is pleased to announce that at the Company's Annual General Meeting held earlier today, the proposed resolutions were all duly passed.
For further information contact:
Critical Metals plc
Danilo Lange, CEO
c/o
critical@stbridespartners.co.uk
St Brides Partners Ltd
Financial PR
Ana Ribeiro/Charlotte Page
critical@stbridespartners.co.uk
About Critical Metals
London-listed Critical Metals plc is focused on identifying low CAPEX and OPEX brown-field projects with near-term production and cash-flow, concentrating on minerals that have strategic importance to future global economic growth. In line with this, and with an off-take partner already in place, the Company is currently focused on recommencing production at the formerly producing Molulu Copper/Cobalt Project in the Katangan Copperbelt in Democratic Republic of Congo ('DRC').

company_30s.what_they_do: Critical Metals Plc mines and processes key materials like lithium, cobalt and rare‑earth elements that power batteries, electronics and green‑energy tech.
company_30s.why_they_matter: These metals are the backbone of the electric‑vehicle and renewable‑energy supply chains, and shortages drive strong demand for any company that can reliably deliver them.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a precarious phase – board changes, a suspension of trading, and court documents pointing to insolvency. The share price has collapsed to effectively zero, indicating the stock is likely halted or delisted and the company is fighting to stay afloat.

rns_narrative: Financial restructuring is the main storyline: new convertible loan notes, board reshuffles and an AGM all point to attempts at raising capital and reorganising debt.
Legal pressure is mounting: the trading suspension and court filings suggest the company is under insolvency scrutiny, which could lead to liquidation if no relief is found.
A key shareholder, NIU Invest, has increased its stake to nearly 70%, signalling that someone still sees value and may push for a turnaround.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: A quiet, almost empty discussion punctuated by a few bearish voices and a handful of skeptical bull comments.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Very few bulls – one trader joking that the market is ignoring the company, implying there’s a hidden story.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears focus on the insolvency rumours, trading halt and the fact that the stock is trading at zero, painting a picture of inevitable collapse.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from tentative curiosity to outright pessimism as the court case progresses.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, so a sudden positive development would catch the market by surprise.

near_term_catalysts: A court decision that lifts the trading suspension or confirms a restructuring plan.
The successful issuance of the new convertible loan notes, which would inject fresh cash.
A headline‑making supply contract with a major battery or EV manufacturer.
Any announcement that NIU Invest is injecting additional equity or taking a controlling stake.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a court ruling allowing trading to resume, a spike in trading volume on a resumed listing, or a clear statement that the company has secured new capital. Also monitor NIU Invest’s public filings for a capital call or takeover plan.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share sits at the very bottom of its cycle – it has already capitulated. The APEX score shows tight compression, meaning a reversal could come quickly once a catalyst materialises, but the window is still wide because the market is waiting on legal outcomes.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company clears its insolvency hurdles and lands new funding, the stock could climb back to a level that is roughly halfway between its current price and its historic peak. That would translate to a 2‑3× return on the money you invest now, because the upside is far larger than the remaining downside.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the court keeps the suspension or the company is liquidated, the price could stay at zero forever. The worst‑case scenario is a total loss of the capital you put in.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You can lose only what you invest (the current price is effectively zero). The potential upside is at least double the investment if the share returns to half its former high, giving a risk‑reward ratio of 1:2 or better.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter as soon as trading resumes or a positive announcement is made. Use a small position size (e.g., 5% of your portfolio) because the price is still highly volatile. Place a stop just below the current price or at a 20% drop from the entry point to protect against a final collapse.
playbook.if_watching: Set a watch on the court docket for a ruling that lifts the suspension. Also monitor the volume on any resumed trading day; a spike above 200% of the average daily volume would be a strong confirmation.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, the stock will be wiped out. In that case, cut losses immediately and move on.

ai_insights.bottom_line: The share is at a deep crash with no market chatter – a classic contrarian set‑up. If a restructuring or new capital injection comes through, the upside is huge compared to the near‑zero downside.
